exercises.description: Configure @nestjs/throttler with different limits for different endpoints (stricter for auth, relaxed for reads). Add response caching for public endpoints with cache invalidation on writes.
exercises.hint: Use the multi-throttler config with named throttlers. Apply @Throttle() per endpoint for custom limits. Use CACHE_MANAGER to manually invalidate cache after creates/updates.

problems.problem: Uploaded file is missing after server restart
problems.cause: Files are stored in the container's filesystem. Container restarts wipe the filesystem.
problems.solution: Use external storage (S3, GCS) for production. For local development, mount a volume. Never store uploads in the application directory.

problems.problem: Cache returns stale data after updates
problems.cause: The cache TTL hasn't expired, so the old data is served even though the database was updated.
problems.solution: Manually invalidate cache after write operations using `cacheManager.del(key)` or `cacheManager.reset()`. Use cache keys that include relevant filter parameters.

problems.problem: Rate limiter blocks legitimate users
problems.cause: Rate limits are too restrictive, or all users behind a load balancer share the same IP.
problems.solution: Tune rate limits based on actual usage patterns. Use X-Forwarded-For header to get the real client IP behind proxies. Consider user-based rate limiting instead of IP-based for authenticated endpoints.

4.7.3.intuition: Testing, documentation, and production config are like the quality assurance, user manual, and safety features of a car. Tests ensure the engine runs correctly. Swagger docs are the owner's manual for API consumers. Production config (helmet, compression, health checks) are the airbags and seatbelts that protect your app in the real world.

exercises.title: Write Comprehensive Tests
exercises.description: Write unit tests for PostsService (all CRUD methods, including error cases) and e2e tests for the full posts API (create, read, update, delete, pagination, search). Aim for 80%+ coverage on the service layer.
exercises.hint: Mock PrismaService for unit tests. Use the real database for e2e tests with cleanup in beforeAll/afterAll. Test auth scenarios: unauthenticated, wrong user, admin access.

exercises.title: Add Swagger Documentation
exercises.description: Document the entire API with Swagger. Add descriptions to all endpoints, DTOs, and response types. Include example values. Test the Swagger UI at /docs to ensure all endpoints are properly documented and testable.
exercises.hint: Use @ApiTags for grouping, @ApiOperation for descriptions, @ApiProperty for DTO fields, @ApiBearerAuth for protected endpoints. Enable the Swagger CLI plugin in nest-cli.json for automatic DTO detection.

exercises.title: Production-Ready Deployment
exercises.description: Create a Docker setup with multi-stage builds. Add health check endpoints. Configure compression and helmet. Set up environment-specific logging. Create a docker-compose.yml with the API and PostgreSQL. Verify the app starts and passes health checks in Docker.
exercises.hint: Use the Dockerfile template from this lesson. Add `@nestjs/terminus` for health checks. Test with `docker-compose up --build` and hit the health endpoint.

problems.problem: E2E tests fail with database connection errors
problems.cause: The test environment doesn't have a running database, or the DATABASE_URL in .env.test points to the wrong database.
problems.solution: Create a .env.test file with a test-specific database URL. Use docker-compose to spin up a test database. Or use an in-memory database for faster tests.

problems.problem: Swagger doesn't show DTO properties
problems.cause: The Swagger CLI plugin isn't enabled, and DTOs don't have @ApiProperty decorators.
problems.solution: Either add @ApiProperty to every DTO field, or enable the CLI plugin in nest-cli.json: `"plugins": ["@nestjs/swagger"]`. The plugin automatically reads class-validator decorators.

problems.problem: Application crashes on shutdown with pending connections
problems.cause: Shutdown hooks aren't enabled, or services don't properly clean up (close database connections, stop background jobs).
problems.solution: Call `app.enableShutdownHooks()` in bootstrap. Implement OnModuleDestroy in services that hold connections. For Prisma, call $disconnect in onModuleDestroy.

further_reading.title: NestJS Deployment Documentation
further_reading.note: Covers various deployment strategies including Docker, serverless, and traditional hosting.
